title: Treasure of the Hidden Planet

'''''Treasure of the Hidden Planet''''' is an animated film released on DVD in 2004. It is a feature-length compilation of the [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] episodes "[[Tail of the Roon Comets]]", "[[The Roon Games]]", "[[Across the Roon Sea]]," and "[[The Frozen Citadel]]."

==Development==
===Production===
In 1997, episodes 5–8 were edited together to make ''[[The Pirates and the Prince]]'', and in 2004 episodes 10–13 were edited together to make ''Treasure of the Hidden Planet''.<ref>{{CargoBay|url=item-detail/7141|text=Cargo Bay: Droids: The Pirates and the Prince}}</ref><ref>{{Rebelscum|url=droids-ewoks.asp|text=Droids and Ewoks: A Home Video History}}</ref> ''The Pirates and the Prince'' combines four of the five episodes of the Mon Julpa arc into one story, while ''Treasure of the Hidden Planet'' combines the four episodes of the Mungo Baobab arc into one story. The original opening credits and theme song are not included as part of the films, and were replaced with simple credit sequences and new scores by [[Marco D'Ambrosio]].

Besides cutting the theme song of the series, many other small modifications were made for the home video releases, most notably changes to the soundtrack.<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.genreonline.net/SWDroids_DVD.html|text=Star Wars Animated Adventures Droids The Pirates And The Prince & Treasure Of The Hidden Planet Double Feature DVD Review|author=Rivera, Mark A.|work=Genreonline.net|archivedate=20050114075106}}</ref> The song that R2-D2 plays on the jukebox is now the song that the Max Rebo Band plays in ''The Pirates and the Prince''. A new prologue is narrated by [[Alex Lindsay]] (digital effects artist for [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''The Phantom Menace'']]), as the voice of Mungo Baobab.<ref>{{IMDb|url=name/nm0512181|text=Alex Lindsay}}</ref> The opening sequence of ''Treasure of the Hidden Planet'' features clips from the television special "[[The Great Heep]]," although the complete special isn't included. These omissions and the added dialog were intended to make the story into a consistant whole, in order to give it more of a feature-film feeling.

==Release and reception==
[[File:Droids_double_feature.jpg|thumb|150px|The cover of the 2004 DVD release of ''Star Wars: Droids'']]

''Treasure of the Hidden Planet'' was released on [[Wikipedia:DVD region code|Region 1]] [[Wikipedia:DVD|DVD]] on [[November 23]], [[2004]] under the title ''Star Wars Animated Adventures: Droids''. It contained both ''The Pirates and the Prince'' and ''Treasure of the Hidden Planet''.<ref name="SWArchive-A Closer Look at the Ewoks & Droids DVDs">{{SWArchive|url=eu/tv/classic/news20040826.html|text=A Closer Look at the ''Ewoks & Droids'' DVDs}}</ref> These compilation films were later released in several Region 4 countries, such as Brazil (the Brazilian edition was released in [[2005]] with the Region 1 and 4 codes on the same disc) and Australia.
